text: A study on antioxidant and antidiabetic activities of novel two pyrazole derivatives Abstract: Two novel two pyrazole derivatives viz., 2-(5-methyl-1H-pyrazole-3-carbonyl)-N-phenylhydrazine-1-carboxamide (Pyz-1) and 4-amino-5-(5-methyl-1H-pyrazol-3-yl)-4H-1,2,4-triazole-3-thiol (Pyz-2) have been synthesized and characterized by 13 C-NMR, 1 H-NMR, FT-IR and mass spectrometry. The complete molecular structures optimization, electronic and thermodynamic properties of Pyz-1 and Pyz-2 in gas phase and aqueous solution were predicted by using hybrid B3LYP method with the 6-311++G** basis sets. The title compounds Pyz-1 and Pyz-2 were evaluated for their antidiabetic, antioxidant and xanthine oxidase inhibition activities. For antidiabetic studies, both Pyz-1 and Pyz-2 showed potential inhibition towards α-glucosidase and α-amylase enzymes with IC50 values of 75.62 ± 0.56 and 95.85 ± 0.92 µM, 119.3 ± 0.75 and 120.2 ± 0.68 µM respectively. The IC50 values for both enzymes are compared with that of standard Acarbose, which showed IC50 values of 72.58 ± 0.68 µM and 115.6 ± 0.574 µM for α-glucosidase and α-amylase enzymes respectively. The inhibitory potential towards xanthine oxidase activity was studied by analyzing the IC50 values of Pyz-1 and Pyz-2 towards the enzyme which showed that Pyz-1 and Pyz-2 has excellent inhibitory activity towards the enzyme with IC50 values of 24.32 ± 0.78 and 10.75 ± 0.54 µM respectively. The antioxidants activity studies clearly indicate that the title compounds Pyz-1 and Pyz-2 have excellent scavenging and free radical activities. Molecular docking studies have been done between the title compounds and the α-glucosidase and α-amylase enzymes to check their binding modes and energies towards inhibition of enzyme activity.
